2603.16434.title: From Natural Language to Executable Option Strategies via Large Language Models
2603.16434.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at general code generation, yet translating natural-language trading intents into correct option strategies remains challenging. Real-world option design requires reasoning over massive, multi-dimensional option chain data with strict constraints, which often overwhelms direct generation methods. We introduce the Option Query Language (OQL), a domain-specific intermediate representation that abstracts option markets into high-level primitives under grammatical rules, enabling LLMs to function as reliable semantic parsers rather than free-form programmers. OQL queries are then validated and executed deterministically by an engine to instantiate executable strategies. We also present a new dataset for this task and demonstrate that our neuro-symbolic pipeline significantly improves execution accuracy and logical consistency over direct baselines.
2603.16434.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在一般代碼生成方面表現出色，但將自然語言交易意圖轉換為正確的期權策略仍然具有挑戰性。現實世界中的期權設計需要對龐大且多維的期權鏈數據進行推理，並且具有嚴格的約束，這常常使直接生成方法不堪重負。我們引入了期權查詢語言（OQL），這是一種領域特定的中間表示，將期權市場抽象為高級原語，遵循語法規則，使LLMs能夠作為可靠的語義解析器，而不是自由形式的程序員。然後，OQL查詢由引擎進行驗證並確定性執行，以實現可執行的策略。我們還為這項任務提出了一個新的數據集，並展示了我們的神經符號管道在執行準確性和邏輯一致性方面顯著改善了直接基準。
2603.16434.author: Haochen Luo et.al.
2603.16434.authors: Haochen Luo, Zhengzhao Lai, Junjie Xu, Yifan Li, Tang Pok Hin, Yuan Zhang, Chen Liu
